# Aura

A standalone, reusable **design language** — raw HTML + CSS + vanilla JS, no
framework and no build step. Aura is decoupled from any single app: this repo is
the home of the language, and downstream projects consume it as a prebuilt
**two-file bundle** (`dist/`) or as a **git submodule** vendoring the sources.

It is built for spatial depth and tactile interaction:

1. **Layers & depth** — a first-class elevation system where each level moves
   shadow, backdrop-blur, surface brightness, and a top-edge light catch together.
   Every surface is *see-through* glass: content scrolling out of its container
   passes **behind** the chrome above it (a header, a toolbar) and stays visible
   through the glass — surfaces separate by depth, never by opaque paint.
2. **Theming** — re-skin everything from three knobs: a primary color, a
   secondary color, and a wallpaper (image, gradient, or a generated *aurora*
   mesh). Dark by default; light supported.
3. **Proximity glow** — interactivity is discoverable without clutter: a halo
   blooms and the control leans toward the cursor as it nears *any* interactive
   widget, and glass surfaces catch a pointer-tracked sheen. Glow + magnetism
   apply to everything clickable, and only to what's clickable.
4. **Rich context menus** — icons, descriptions, shortcuts, badges, checkbox/
   radio state, submenus, and danger styling — all authored declaratively.
5. **Declarative markup** — high-level `aura-*` elements describe UI directly,
   instead of hand-assembling low-level markup.
6. **Space economy** — the content workspace is maximized: chrome that isn't
   continuously needed (primary nav, the footer) is summoned on demand — hover a
   stash zone, hit a shortcut, scroll to the end — and yields its space back when
   done, sized to only what it needs.
7. **Responsive by default** — one adaptive layout reads and works across the
   full screen spectrum: phone, laptop/monitor, half-snapped windows, and
   ultrawide. Sizing is container-relative (not viewport-locked), text holds a
   readable measure on wide screens, and chrome collapses gracefully on small
   ones — no per-target rebuild.

**Demo showcase (local):** from a clone, run **`just run`** and open
**http://localhost:8753/** — it lands on the demo hub (`demo/home.html`) and
every demo page carries the full chrome. The generated pages are also
browsable directly at **[demo/index.html](demo/index.html)**. (There is no
hosted site: the repo is private and the GitHub Pages deploy was deliberately
skipped — all consumers are internal for the foreseeable future.) Design
rationale lives in **[docs/design/](docs/design/README.md)**.

## Quick start — the `dist/` bundle (recommended)

The whole design language ships as **two prebuilt files**: `dist/aura.min.css`
and `dist/aura.min.js` (unminified twins `dist/aura.css` / `dist/aura.js`).
Copy them (or the whole repo) next to your page — here vendored as `aura/` —
and you're done; no install, no build step:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" data-aura-theme="dark">
  <head>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="aura/dist/aura.min.css" />
  </head>
  <body>
    <aura-app>
      <aura-card elevation="2">
        <aura-stack gap="3">
          <h3>Hello, Aura</h3>
          <aura-field label="Email" hint="We'll never share it.">
            <input type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
          </aura-field>
          <aura-button variant="primary" icon="check">Save</aura-button>
        </aura-stack>
      </aura-card>
    </aura-app>

    <script src="aura/dist/aura.min.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>
```

[`dist/example.html`](dist/example.html) is a working kitchen-sink page built
from exactly these two files — open it for a copyable reference of buttons,
cards, dialogs, menus, tabs, and selects running off the bundle alone.

**Subresource integrity.** When serving the bundle from a CDN or another
origin, add `integrity`/`crossorigin` attributes using the current sha384
hashes from [`dist/SRI.txt`](dist/SRI.txt) (regenerated by every build):

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.example.com/aura/aura.min.css"
      integrity="sha384-<copy from dist/SRI.txt>" crossorigin="anonymous" />
<script src="https://cdn.example.com/aura/aura.min.js"
        integrity="sha384-<copy from dist/SRI.txt>" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
```

**Opt-in theme packs** live in [`dist/themes/`](dist/themes/) (`aqua-aero`,
`extra-depth`, `flat-primary`, `frutiger-aero`, `modern-flat`, `retro-pc`,
`warm-dusk`). Each pack is scoped to
`[data-aura-theme-name="<pack>"]`, so re-skinning is two steps: link the pack
*after* the main stylesheet, **and** activate it by setting the matching
attribute on the scope you want re-skinned (use `:root`/`<html>` for the whole
page):

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="…/dist/aura.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="…/dist/themes/warm-dusk.css" />
<html data-aura-theme-name="warm-dusk"> … </html>
```

The link alone re-skins nothing until the attribute names the pack.

To preview everything the language offers, serve this repo with `just run`
(which runs `dev-server.py`) and browse the 18 reference pages under `/demo/`.
Use `just run`, not a bare `python3 -m http.server`: the
`theme-authoring.html` demo POSTs to a `/__save-theme` endpoint that only
`dev-server.py` implements (plain `http.server` returns 501), and the dev server
also disables caching so edits show on reload.

## Consuming from source (git submodule)

```sh
git submodule add <this-repo-url> vendor/aura
```

Then either point at the bundle inside the submodule
(`vendor/aura/dist/aura.min.css` + `vendor/aura/dist/aura.min.js`), or load the
sources directly: one CSS entry (`vendor/aura/css/aura.css` — a cascade-layer
barrel that imports everything) plus the classic scripts you use, in dependency
order. The runtime is split into one file per element (~50 scripts under
`js/`); `core.js` then `element-base.js` always come first. The quick-start
page above needs exactly these six (each element self-documents its load order
as `core.js → element-base.js → <element>.js`):

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="vendor/aura/css/aura.css" />

<!-- classic scripts, dependency order, no build step -->
<script src="vendor/aura/js/core.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/aura/js/element-base.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/aura/js/aura-app.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/aura/js/aura-card.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/aura/js/aura-field.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/aura/js/aura-button.js"></script>

<!-- Optional (this static page doesn't need them): theme.js enables runtime
     theme/mode switching + persistence, glow.js the proximity-glow halo. Add
     them after core.js, before element-base.js, only when you use those features. -->
<!-- <script src="vendor/aura/js/theme.js"></script> -->
<!-- <script src="vendor/aura/js/glow.js"></script> -->
```

Layout elements (`aura-stack`, `aura-row`, `aura-grid`) are CSS-only — no
script needed. For the per-element file names beyond these, see the script
block of any `demo/*.html` page, or skip the bookkeeping and use the two-file
bundle. (Loading sources individually is mainly useful when hacking on Aura
itself or cherry-picking a minimal payload.)

For the complete ordered runtime manifest — every module's load position,
direct dependencies, and CSS cascade layer, plus the supported full-bundle and
cherry-pick recipes — see [`docs/dependency-manifest.md`](docs/dependency-manifest.md)
(machine-readable twin: [`dist/dependency-manifest.json`](dist/dependency-manifest.json)).
Both are generated from the build's single source of truth and drift-checked on
every build, so they never fall out of step with the shipped bundle.

**Opt-in extras.** A standalone **nav-extras** module
(`dist/aura-nav-extras.{css,js}` — accent-colour picker, sticky unread chip,
chord navigation, recently-visited panel, copy-link overlay, sort controls) ships
*outside* the default bundle so no page pays for it unless it uses it. Load its
two tags **after** the core bundle on the pages that need it; see the
[Opt-in extras section](docs/dependency-manifest.md#opt-in-extras-load-on-top-of-the-core-bundle)
of the dependency manifest for the recipe and SRI hashes.

## Consuming Aura via the Dependency Channel

A fourth path, alongside `dist/`, the submodule, and npm-git above: pin an
exact version from a **published GitHub Release** and let a small stdlib
engine resolve, download, checksum-verify, and vendor it — no clone of this
repo, no toolchain, just `gh`/`curl` and a checksum tool.

Every release since **v3.540** publishes the Dependency Channel trio —
`aura-v{X.Y.Z}.tar.gz` + `release.json` + `SHA256SUMS` — plus the legacy
zip/loose `dist/` assets. If your project runs Grimoire's
`grm-sync-deps` engine, declare the pin in your own repo's `vendor.toml`:

```toml
[deps.aura]
repo = "rhohn94/design-language"
channel = "stable"
version = "3.555.0"                 # the pin — sync fetches exactly this tag
artifact = "aura-v3.555.0.tar.gz"
dest = "lib/third-party/aura"
kind = "asset-bundle"
strip_components = 1                # tarball entries are namespaced aura-vX.Y.Z/
```

then sync it:

```sh
python3 .claude/skills/grm-sync-deps/sync_deps.py --dep aura
```

Without that tooling, verify the trio by hand — this actually works against
the real, currently-published release:

```sh
gh release download v3.555.0 --repo rhohn94/design-language
shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS   # aura-v3.555.0.tar.gz: OK / release.json: OK
```

Starting with v3.556, the tarball *additionally* ships `tokens.resolved.json` +
`tools/aura_tokens.py` + `tools/aura_conformance.py` (new — earlier releases'
tarballs carry the trio but not these three files), so native/egui
consumers can regenerate platform token files from the tarball alone, with
no checkout of this repo. For "what changed and do I need to act," read the
curated notes at `docs/release-notes/v{X.Y}.md` (this **is** the GitHub
Release body). `tools/check_release_current.py`
(`just check-release-current`) is a standing guard confirming the channel
hasn't gone stale.

Full field-by-field `release.json` reference, the vendor.toml schema, and a
verified walkthrough: **[`docs/design/distribution-design.md`
§3c](docs/design/distribution-design.md#3c-the-dependency-channel--pin-sync-and-verify-a-release-1062)**.

## Framework bindings (React / Vue / Svelte)

Aura's core is framework-free custom elements, and **optional** thin adapters
for React, Vue 3, and Svelte live in [`bindings/`](bindings/README.md) —
typed React wrappers + hooks, a Vue compiler predicate, and a Svelte event
action.

**React quick setup:**

```js
import "@aura-design/core/style";              // CSS
import "@aura-design/core/dist/aura.js";        // runtime (registers aura-* elements)
import { AuraButton, createAuraComponent } from "@aura-design/core/bindings/react";
import { useAuraDarkMode } from "@aura-design/core/bindings/react/hooks";
import { AuraAlert } from "@aura-design/core/bindings/react/css-class-wrappers";

// Wrap any element not yet in the built-in set (forwards refs, reflects boolean props,
// wires custom-event handlers via the events prop):
const AuraMyWidget = createAuraComponent("aura-my-widget");
```

For step-by-step setup for React, Vue 3, and Svelte — including framework-specific
config, event handling, and hook reference — see **[`docs/quickstart.md`](docs/quickstart.md)**.
For the complete binding API (all wrappers, hooks, types, CSS-class components), see
**[`bindings/README.md`](bindings/README.md)**.

> **npm:** the package is named **`@aura-design/core`** (`package.json`
> exposes `@aura-design/core/dist/*`, `@aura-design/core/esm/*`,
> `@aura-design/core/css/*`, `@aura-design/core/js/*`, `@aura-design/core/templates/*`,
> `@aura-design/core/tokens` (exports `auraTokenVars` — live `var(--aura-*)` references — and `auraTokens` — resolved values — for any ESM consumer, not just React; see `docs/quickstart.md §Vanilla HTML / CSS tokens`),
> and the enumerated binding subpaths
> `@aura-design/core/bindings/{react,vue,svelte}` (plus `bindings/react/hooks`
> and `bindings/react/jsx.d.ts`) via its `exports` map — the bindings are
> specific subpaths, not a `bindings/*` glob).
>
> **The package is not yet published to npm.** Install it from git — every
> documented specifier resolves identically:
>
> ```sh
> npm install github:rhohn94/design-language
> ```
>
> — or consume via `dist/` / the submodule, or via the versioned
> `aura-vX.Y.tar.gz` release asset. Do **not** `npm install aura` — that bare
> registry name belongs to an unrelated third-party project. The first
> registry publish is human-gated and follows
> [`docs/npm-publish-runbook.md`](docs/npm-publish-runbook.md) (rationale:
> `docs/design/distribution-design.md` §3b); until it lands, docs are held to
> this reality by the `check-registry-claims` gate.

### Server-rendered apps (Askama + HTMX)

Aura is designed for server-rendered HTML that swaps DOM fragments with HTMX:

- **One CSS entry**, `css/aura.css`, using native cascade `@layer`. Consumer CSS
  authored *without* `@layer` always overrides Aura, so adoption never fights
  your styles.
- **No per-element JS binding.** Glow and menus use event delegation; glow keeps
  a live registry via a `MutationObserver`; custom elements enhance themselves in
  `connectedCallback`. So anything HTMX swaps in **just works** with no re-init.
- If you insert DOM through a non-HTMX path, call `Aura.refresh()` to re-scan.

## Theming at runtime

```js
Aura.theme.setPrimary("#7654f5");
Aura.theme.setSecondary("#22d3ee");
Aura.theme.setMode("light");            // "dark" | "light" | "auto"
Aura.theme.setWallpaper("aurora");      // or a url()/gradient string, or "none"
Aura.theme.apply({ primary, secondary, wallpaper, mode, scope });
```

These only write CSS custom properties / the theme attribute, so changes survive
HTMX swaps. All design tokens are plain `--aura-*` custom properties (declared in
`css/tokens.css`, which is generated from the canonical `tokens.json` source —
see `docs/design/token-source-design.md`) and can be overridden per scope. The
ready-made theme packs under `dist/themes/` (source: `css/themes/`) are plain
token-override stylesheets — each scoped to `[data-aura-theme-name="<pack>"]`,
so set that attribute on the scope to activate the pack (see the install
snippet above) — that you can also use as authoring templates.

## Other runtime APIs

`window.Aura` exposes several utility namespaces alongside `Aura.theme`:

| Namespace | What it does |
|---|---|
| `Aura.format` | Locale-aware number formatting — `number`, `currency`, `percent`, `compact` (all `Intl`-backed) |
| `Aura.color` | Color-model utilities — WCAG contrast, `parseColor`, hex/RGB/HSL/OKLCH conversions |
| `Aura.entrance` | Entrance choreography — `replay(root?)` re-triggers animations after HTMX swaps |
| `Aura.shell` | Sidebar drawer control — `open`, `close`, `toggle`, `isOpen` (programmatic, without a toggle button) |
| `Aura.dialog` | Dialog control — `open`, `close`, `closeAll` (focus-managed, opener-aware) |
| `Aura.menu` | Menu control — `open`, `openAtAnchor`, `closeAll` |
| `Aura.copy` | Copy-to-clipboard — `copyText`, `injectCodeButtons`, `textFor`, `announce` |
| `Aura.form` | Form re-scan — `refresh(root?)` after dynamic injection |
| `Aura.tokens` | Token derivation — `link`, `unlink`, `isLinked`, `source`, `links`, `setSource`, `detach`, `applyLinked` |
| `Aura.icons` | Named icon registry — `register(name, markup)`, `unregister(name)` |
| `Aura.lightbox` | Image lightbox — `open(src, alt?, caption?, opener?)`, `close()` |
| `Aura.nav` | Nav header i18n — `nav.strings` object with 30+ localizable keys |

All members are typed as `AuraGlobal` in `dist/aura.d.ts` (importable as
`import type { AuraGlobal } from "@aura-design/core"`). See
[`docs/quickstart.md` §Programmatic APIs](docs/quickstart.md#programmatic-apis-framework-agnostic)
for worked examples.

## Repository layout

```
dist/         prebuilt bundle — aura(.min).css/.js, SRI.txt, example.html, themes/
css/          source stylesheets — aura.css (cascade-layer barrel) + per-subsystem files + generated tokens.css
js/           runtime — ~50 classic scripts (core, theme, glow, element-base, one file per element)
bindings/     optional React / Vue / Svelte adapters (see bindings/README.md)
catalog/      per-component metadata (component.json) for downstream discovery
templates/    page-layout blueprints — app shell, form/list/content-page/detail/settings/dashboard/error pages
demo/         18 reference demo pages (rendered from site/), also copyable starters
dist-src/     authored sources for generated dist/ pages (e.g. example.html/css)
site/         Rust/askama generator that renders demo/*.html from a master template
tools/        consumer tooling — token export, conformance checker, doc/demo gates
docs/design/  per-subsystem design docs
```

## Building from source (contributors)

Consuming Aura needs **no toolchain** — the prebuilt `dist/` bundle is committed.
*Rebuilding* it (`just build`) and running the release gate (`just check`) do have
prerequisites:

- **Python 3** — the bundler, token codegen, and the stdlib gates.
- **Node + npm**, plus a Playwright Chromium (`npm install` then
  `npx playwright install chromium`) — the in-browser test, snapshot, a11y, css,
  and layout gates.
- **Rust toolchain (`cargo`/`rustup`)** — `just build` runs `gen-demos`, which
  renders `demo/*.html` from the askama master template via the `site/` crate
  (`cargo run`). There is no fallback, so `just build`/`just check` require cargo
  on PATH even though pure *consumption* does not.

## Browser support

Targets evergreen browsers. Uses modern CSS (`color-mix()`, OKLCH, cascade
`@layer`, `backdrop-filter`) with graceful fallbacks: surfaces fall back to
opaque tints where `backdrop-filter` is unavailable, and `aura-*` elements stay
styled even if custom elements don't upgrade.
